description_long: This is a rustic log cabin with two distinct sections: one constructed from horizontally stacked logs and the other from rough stone. The cabin features a gabled roof with dark, weathered shingles, some of which show patches of moss or lichen. The front facade has a wooden door and two windows, one with shutters and the other with a simple frame, both showing signs of age. A stone chimney protrudes from the lower roof, and climbing vegetation is visible along the stone wall. The windows and door are framed with wood, and the overall structure appears sturdy and aged, blending natural materials with a traditional design. The cabin sits low to the ground and is surrounded by no visible foundation or porch.
description: A rustic log and stone cabin with a gabled shingle roof, wooden door, and aged windows, featuring a stone chimney and climbing vegetation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance door, two windows (one with shutters), and the transition between log and stone construction.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the log wall, a single window with shutters, and the full gable roof profile.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the stone wall, a window with a wooden frame, and the climbing vegetation.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the log section, the stone chimney, and the roof's shingle texture with moss patches.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with overlapping shingle tiles and a stone chimney, moderate overhangs, and visible moss patches
attribute_window_arrangement: Three windows: one large window with shutters on the log section front, one medium window above the door, and one small window on the stone section side; arrangement is asymmetrical and irregular
attribute_door_placement: Single wooden door centered on the front facade between the two main sections
